name: Joseph Haydn
abstract: Franz Joseph Haydn ( HY-dən; German: [ˈfʁants ˈjoːzɛf ˈhaɪdn̩] ; 31 March 1732 – 31 May 1809) was an Austrian composer of the Classical period. He was pivotal in the evolution of chamber music forms like the string quartet and piano trio.

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: There was a family called Esterházy in Austria and they were like the patrons of Josef Haydn, the composer. Haydn wrote the first trumpet concerto that was written, and they hired me to play this.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roland Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: Look at Haydn, Franz Joseph Haydn, the “Farewell Symphony.” There’s an example of how afraid they were.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roland Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000
